items: Up/down buttons (or drag) correctly disabled/constrained for tracks at the boundary of their kind group
Audio tracks with 2+ tracks can be reordered (currently broken)
Reorder across kind boundaries is prevented — visual tracks stay above audio tracks
Drag handle replaces arrow buttons in TrackLabel hover state
Dragging a track visually reorders it within its kind group and persists the change
No interference between track drag and existing clip drag or external drop functionality
Track order survives save/load cycle (sortTracksByKind preserves relative order within kind)

unresolved_flags.concern: Track reordering: Replacing the arrow buttons with a drag-only handle removes the current keyboard-accessible reorder path, and the plan's drag setup only mentions `PointerSensor`.
unresolved_flags.evidence: Current reorder controls are keyboard-focusable buttons in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TrackLabel.tsx:196-220`. The plan removes them in Step 5, while Step 7 proposes a nested `DndContext` with `PointerSensor` only, even though the app-level context already wires a `KeyboardSensor` in `src/app/App.tsx:47-53`.

unresolved_flags.concern: Track reordering: The plan relies on manual validation only and does not add automated tests for the same-kind boundary rules or save/load persistence.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The current defect is in pure reorder logic at `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts:286-308`, and persistence also depends on load-time regrouping in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts:320-333`. This repo already has vitest coverage for adjacent video-editor serialization logic in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/serialize.test.ts:1-58`, so omitting tests leaves the subtle boundary behavior unguarded.

rationale: Both unresolved flags are executor-level implementation details, not planning gaps. FLAG-001 (keyboard accessibility) is a one-line addition of KeyboardSensor alongside PointerSensor in the nested DndContext — the plan's architecture already supports it. FLAG-003 (no automated tests) is a valid concern but the reorder logic is pure and testable; the executor can add a test file during Step 2 or Step 6 without any plan restructuring. The plan itself is well-phased (bugs first, then drag), correctly identifies the root causes, respects the visual-before-audio invariant, and isolates track DnD from clip DnD via nested DndContext. No debt subsystem overlaps or recurring critiques to worry about.
signals_assessment: Iteration 1, weighted score 3.5, no prior trajectory. Two significant flags remain but both are additive implementation details (add a sensor, add a test file) rather than architectural corrections. No plan delta to evaluate, no recurring critiques, no scope creep. Preflight is fully clean. The score reflects the open flags but the plan's structure is stable and doesn't need revision to address them.
warnings: Executor should add KeyboardSensor to the nested DndContext (Step 7) to preserve keyboard reorder accessibility — don't ship pointer-only.
Executor should add at least one vitest file covering same-kind boundary enforcement in handleReorderTrack/handleMoveTrack, following the existing pattern in serialize.test.ts.

settled_decisions.decision: Use a nested DndContext for track reordering rather than the app-level DndContext.
settled_decisions.rationale: Isolates track drag events from clip/shot DnD. The plan correctly identified this as the safer approach given the existing app-level DnD wiring.

settled_decisions.decision: Maintain the visual-before-audio track ordering invariant; reordering is within-kind-group only.
settled_decisions.rationale: Consistent with sortTracksByKind enforcement on load. Cross-kind reordering would require schema and UX changes beyond this scope.

settled_decisions.decision: Phase the work: fix reorder bugs first (Steps 1-3), then layer drag-to-reorder (Steps 4-8).
settled_decisions.rationale: Bug fixes are independently valuable and validate the reorder logic before building the drag UX on top of it.

orchestrator_guidance: First iteration; follow gate recommendation: PROCEED. Verify unresolved flags against the plan and project code before accepting.

signals.idea: Fix track reordering bugs and replace arrow buttons with drag-to-reorder. Current issues: (1) Moving video tracks above/below audio tracks stalls or doesn't work - there's some kind of contradiction in the reorder logic when crossing track kind boundaries. (2) Audio tracks can't be moved up at all. (3) Replace the up/down arrow buttons in TrackLabel with a drag handle so users can drag tracks to reorder them directly.

unresolved_flags.concern: Track reordering: Replacing the arrow buttons with a drag-only handle removes the current keyboard-accessible reorder path, and the plan's drag setup only mentions `PointerSensor`.
Track reordering: The plan relies on manual validation only and does not add automated tests for the same-kind boundary rules or save/load persistence.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 3.5. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 2.
